page: UpCloud GPU-8xCPU-64GB-1xL4 is a KVM-based virtual server configured with an AMD EPYC 9575F processor featuring 8 vCPUs and 8 physical cores on the x86_64 architecture. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 8.0 GB per core. Acceleration is delivered via a single NVIDIA L4 GPU based on the Ada Lovelace architecture, which includes 24 GB of dedicated VRAM. This instance does not include local storage, and it comes with one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark results show top-tier single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core scoring 4092.76 and single-threaded gzip compression reaching 43.94 GB/sec. Multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth fall into the average tier, with a passmark CPU score of 27004.32 and large-block memory read bandwidth of 123.85 GB/sec. For machine learning workloads, the NVIDIA L4 GPU achieves top-tier text generation speeds of 402.56 tokens per second using a small 135M LLM model, while prompt processing for the same model operates at an average tier of 1942.23 tokens per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the server offers a balanced profile for GPU-accelerated workloads that do not require local storage, leveraging shared CPU allocation to optimize resource density. The combination of an AMD EPYC processor and an NVIDIA L4 GPU makes this server suitable for small-scale LLM inference, machine learning model prototyping, data science tasks, and GPU-accelerated compute workloads that rely on external storage systems.

Performance benchmarks highlight top-tier single-core CPU capabilities, with stress-ng single-core scoring 4092.76, and top-tier text generation speeds of 402.56 tokens per second for small LLM models. Multi-core performance and memory bandwidth remain in the average tier. This server is qualitatively cost-efficient for GPU-accelerated workloads that utilize external storage, making it well-suited for machine learning inference, data science tasks, and specialized compute workloads.

tagline: An accelerated virtual server combining AMD EPYC processors and NVIDIA L4 graphics for efficient machine learning inference and compute workloads.
